LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT GARY BELK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF JOHN SANTORI IS SUSTAINED WITH ONE LAST CHANCE TO AMEND WITHIN 10 DAYS TO PLEAD SPECIFIC FACTS TO SHOW: 1) THE TIME AND MANNER OF DISCOVERY, AND 2) THE INABILITY TO HAVE MADE EARLIER DISCOVERY DESPITE REASONABLE DILIGENCE, IN LIGHT OF PARAGRAPHS 10 THROUGH 12, AND 44 OF THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John P. Dwyer of Dwyer & Biggs, 294-7760, appearing for cross defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT GARY BELK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT of John Santori is sustained with one last chance to amend within 10 days to plead specific facts to show: 1) the time and manner of discovery, and 2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence, in light of paragraphs 10 through 12, and 44 of the First Amended Cross Complaint. Order signed in open Court. (301/PJB/DM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
